summary: Slack thread export reused from the upstream CWF gather implementation with grant-first and env-fallback metadata.

notes: Provide a runtime grant when the host supports it, or prepare a Slack bot token plus the normal shell dependencies.
The bot may still need to join the target channel before export succeeds.
Refresh the upstream gather implementation when Slack API handling changes.

detect.failure_hint: Provide `node`, `jq`, and `perl`, or skip the Slack export provider.

healthcheck.failure_hint: The shell dependencies exist but the runtime looks broken. Repair the local toolchain before relying on Slack export.

readiness_checks.summary: Shell runtime dependencies for the upstream Slack export helper are available.
commands: command -v node >/dev/null && command -v jq >/dev/null && command -v perl >/dev/null
readiness_checks.failure_hint: Install or expose `node`, `jq`, and `perl` before using the upstream Slack export helpers.

config_layers.summary: Prefer a runtime-granted Slack capability when the host can mediate access without exposing raw secrets.
Fall back to `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` for ordinary local operator setups.
The operator may still need to add the bot to the workspace or target channel before export succeeds.

version_expectation.constraint: Prefer the current upstream Slack export helper behavior.

notes: See `plugins/cwf/skills/gather/references/slack-export.md` for token and scope requirements.

when_missing: Stop cleanly when private Slack access is unavailable instead of inventing thread contents.
Ask for an exported artifact or a different source when the required grant or bot token is absent.
when_version_mismatch: Treat Slack API or formatting drift as a manual review point before trusting converted thread output.

host_notes: Current `charness` v1 sync materializes a support reference, not the upstream executable scripts themselves. Hosts still need an equivalent runtime path to execute Slack export.
This manifest is transitional metadata only. The intended long-term model is `charness`-owned Slack gather provider runtime, not an external plugin runtime dependency.
